failure_reason: Proposed INV20 replacement (e.session >= ob_primary[e.market]) fails immediately after OBViewChange: pre-existing archiver records have session < new ob_primary. Also lacking DA safety justification for OB archiver GC.

proposal.claim: OB archiver stores grow without bound because no GC action exists for ob_archiver_store. Adding session-scoped OB archiver GC is safe because all consumers query OBArchiverSeqsForSession with current session only.

critical_issues.description: The proposed INV20 replacement conjunct is NOT an invariant. After OBViewChange(m), ob_primary[m] increments to S+1 while ob_archiver_store retains pre-existing session S records (ob_archiver_store is UNCHANGED by OBViewChange at line 452). Those records have e.session = S < ob_primary[e.market] = S+1, violating the proposed invariant immediately without any new ingest.

medium_issues.description: OB archiver GC lacks DA safety justification. Original spec intentionally retains OB archiver data indefinitely (line 997). CE has checkpoint/L1 anchor path; OB does not. Removing old-session OB archiver data without equivalent external persistence mechanism is unjustified.

judgment.ruling: Reviewer is correct. The proposed INV20 replacement conjunct is not an invariant. Pre-existing archiver records from session S survive OBViewChange intact: ob_archiver_store is UNCHANGED by OBViewChange (line 452), so any effect ingested during session S remains in the store after ob_primary advances to S+1. Those records have e.session = S < ob_primary[e.market] = S+1, violating the proposed invariant immediately and without any new ingest.
required_for_resubmission: Either remove the INV20 conjunct entirely and rely only on the original CE-only conjunct with an updated comment, OR reformulate so the invariant does not require e.session >= ob_primary[e.market] for records that predate the current view

Provide trust-model or spec-level justification that old-session OB archiver data is safe to discard (i.e., cite an external persistence mechanism equivalent to CE's L1/checkpoint anchor), or withdraw the GC action and document the asymmetry as intentional
